title: Full translation of vetted content (high-risk; needs translation review)
context: Translated patient-facing health content is itself high-risk: a translation error in an educational oncology resource could mislead advocates and harm the patients they represent. Full translation is explicitly out of scope for v1 and is a future track, gated on: the English content being fully vetted and v1.0 tagged; localisation hooks (task 305) in place; and per-language dual sign-off (oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer fluent in the target language) secured. No specific target languages are enumerated yet — this task will be instantiated per language when a need is confirmed and reviewers are secured.
objective: Produce a fully reviewed translation of the complete vetted English curriculum into a target language, with per-language dual high-tier sign-off (oncologist + patient-advocate reviewer fluent in the target language) — scoped when a specific language and partner are confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: English source content is fully vetted (v1.0 tagged) and localisation hooks (task 305) are in place before translation begins.
Target language and a confirmed partner with advocates who need content in that language are identified before this task is scoped (no translation without a confirmed language need).
Translation is produced by a translator fluent in both English and the target language with subject-matter familiarity in health/research methodology.

Translation is reviewed and signed off by a credentialed oncologist fluent in the target language (recorded in the review ledger): clinical terminology is correct and nothing reads as treatment guidance in the target language.

Translation is reviewed and signed off by an experienced patient advocate fluent in the target language (recorded in the review ledger): content is accessible and does not stray into advice in the target language.
All 'not medical advice' notices and provenance footers are translated and present in the target-language version.
Translated content passes the CI build (licence + provenance gate, broken-link check) in the target-language locale.
Translation licence is compatible with CC-BY-4.0; translator attribution is included in the NOTICE file.
resources: PLAN.md (§ Out of scope — translation beyond design hooks in v1; Open Q7 on localisation)
TASKS.md (Backlog / future — Each language re-reviewed; gated like patient-facing content)
output: A translated version of the complete vetted curriculum in the confirmed target language, committed to the repo under the localisation structure from task 305, with per-language dual high-tier sign-offs recorded in the review ledger.
